page: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a virtual machine instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor operating at 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 64 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The processor features a cache hierarchy including 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, vector neural network instructions (AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI), bfloat16 (AVX512_BF16), and cryptographic acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI).

The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with the instance, consisting of a 3840 GB NVMe SSD to support high-capacity local data access. Network connectivity includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In synthetic benchmarks, the server demonstrates strong processing capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 3701.09 places it in the top tier (top 10%), while the multi-core CPU performance score of 117951.61 is rated as strong (top 25%). However, because the CPU allocation is shared, users may experience resource contention under sustained multi-tenant workloads, which represents a key operational tradeoff compared to dedicated-core instances.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU model combined with high resource density, specifically 256.0 GB of RAM and 3840 GB of NVMe storage, provides a cost-effective alternative for workloads requiring large memory and storage footprints without requiring continuous, dedicated CPU execution. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads, large-scale web serving, database hosting, and batch processing tasks that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a high-capacity instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring an AMD EPYC-Turin processor with 64 vCPUs and 64 physical cores under a shared CPU allocation. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the system includes 256.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and a bundled 3840 GB NVMe SSD. Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance (top 10%) and strong multi-core performance (top 25%). The shared CPU architecture offers a cost-effective profile for resource-dense deployments, though it introduces potential resource contention compared to dedicated instances. This server is well-suited for general-purpose applications, database hosting, and large-scale web serving.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a VX1 Cloud Compute instance featuring 64 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-64c-256g-3840s is a shared-CPU instance with 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and a 3840 GB NVMe SSD for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A high-density shared-CPU instance combining sixty-four AMD EPYC cores with substantial memory and fast local NVMe storage.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
